name: Presa de Cornalvo from San Pedro de Mérida — circular through the Parque Natural de Cornalvo
name_original: Presa de Cornalvo desde San Pedro de Mérida — circular por el Parque Natural de Cornalvo
region_intro: Hiking around San Pedro de Mérida offers access to the diverse landscapes of Cornalvo Natural Park, characterized by gentle mountains, rivers, and streams. The terrain features oak dehesas and Mediterranean forests, providing varied environments for outdoor activities. A notable historical feature is the Roman Cornalvo Dam and its reservoir, integrated into the natural park. The region's natural features include several reservoirs and a rich biodiversity, making it a Special Protection Area for Birds. Best hiking trails around San Pedro De Mérida The most popular hiking route is Cornalvo Reservoir Loop, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the Cornalvo Reservoir and its surroundings. Another top favourite among local hikers is Presa de Cornalvo from San Pedro de Mérida — circular through the Parque Natural de Cornalvo, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This trail navigates through the natural park, showcasing its varied ecosystems and the historic Roman dam. Local hikers also love the El Rugidero – circular by the Cornalvo Natural Park, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail leading through the diverse landscapes of Cornalvo Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 14 minutes. Hiking around San Pedro de Mérida is defined by gentle mountains, oak dehesas, and Mediterranean forests, with routes often following rivers and streams. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in San Pedro de Mérida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore San Pedro de Mérida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around San Pedro de Mérida?
A: There are 18 hiking trails around San Pedro de Mérida, offering a variety of experiences within the Cornalvo Natural Park and its surroundings. These routes range from easy strolls to more challenging treks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in this region?
A: Hiking around San Pedro de Mérida primarily features the diverse landscapes of the Cornalvo Natural Park. You can expect gentle mountains, oak dehesas, and Mediterranean forests. Many routes follow rivers and streams, providing varied environments for exploration.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, there are 3 easy hiking routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991631021/cornalvo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.5 km long and takes less than 2 hours to complete, offering pleasant views of the reservoir.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in the area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2359873627/cornalvo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; route is a demanding 25.8 km trail, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991628272/el-rugidero-circular-by-the-cornalvo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero – circular by the Cornalvo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers a 19.8 km trek through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1291247472/presa-de-cornalvo-from-san-pedro-de-merida-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Cornalvo from San Pedro de Mérida&lt;/a&gt; is a 12.3 km circular path that takes you through the natural park and past the historic Roman dam.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical features. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt;, built by the Romans, and its beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6059030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Muelas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is also notable for birdwatching, especially in spring.

Q: Is the area suitable for birdwatching?
A: Yes, the Cornalvo Natural Park is a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), making it an excellent destination for birdwatching. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6059030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Muelas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is particularly known for the spectacular number of birds that can be observed from its viewpoints.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in San Pedro de Mérida?
A: The routes in San Pedro de Mérida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the Cornalvo Natural Park, and the historical significance of sites like the Roman dam.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Pedro de Mérida?
A: The Cornalvo Reservoir and its surroundings are particularly beautiful during leafy and wet seasons, suggesting spring and autumn offer lush scenery. However, the region's gentle mountains and Mediterranean climate generally allow for hiking throughout much of the year, with cooler temperatures in winter and warmer conditions in summer.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Cornalvo Natural Park?
A: The Cornalvo Natural Park is a protected area, and while specific dog policies can vary, generally dogs are permitted on trails but must be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially in a Special Protection Area for Birds. Always check local signage or park regulations for the most current information.

Q: Are there any places to explore near San Pedro de Mérida besides hiking trails?
A: Beyond the hiking trails, the region offers the Aula de la Naturaleza “Cornus Albus” near San Pedro de Mérida, which provides further opportunities to discover the natural richness of the area. The town itself also serves as a gateway to the historical context of the region.
tips: Built around 130 AD, this dam was used to supply water to the ancient Roman city of Augusta Emerita. The reservoir and its surroundings were declared a natural park in 2004, making it an ideal destination for nature and history lovers.
If you decide to explore this route, you will be surrounded by nature, breathtaking views. Enjoy your hike! 🏞️🚶‍♂️🥾🚵🌞
